name: Bastiments y Pic de la Dona - trail run circular through the Natural Park of the Headwaters of Ter and Freser
name_original: Bastiments y Pic de la Dona - trail run circular por el Parque Natural de las Cabeceras del Ter y del Freser
region_intro: Hiking trails around Setcases offer access to the dramatic high-mountain Pyrenean landscape within the Ripollès comarca of Girona, Catalonia. The region is characterized by majestic peaks, many exceeding 2,000 meters, including Bastiments, Pic de la Dona, and Roca Colom. Pine forests and fescue grasslands define the subalpine and alpine zones, with the Ter River originating at Ulldeter. This area provides diverse terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Setcases The most popular hiking route is Bastiments and Pic de la Dona Loop, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 58 minutes to complete. This moderate circular route explores the Natural Park of the Headwaters of Ter and Freser. Another top favourite among local hikers is Costabona Circular from Setcases, a moderate 7.9 miles (12.6 km) path. This trail offers panoramic views of the surrounding high-mountain landscape. Local hikers also love the Roca Colom from Vallter - Circular through the Natural Park of the Headwaters of Ter and Freser, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail leading through the Natural Park of the Headwaters of Ter and Freser, often completed in about 4 hours 38 minutes. Hiking around Setcases is defined by high-mountain Pyrenean landscapes, pine forests, and fescue grasslands. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate ascents to more difficult alpine treks. The routes in Setcases are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Setcases's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Setcases?
A: There are over 90 hiking trails around Setcases, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. More than 4,000 hikers have explored the varied terrain using komoot.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for hiking in Setcases?
A: Hiking in Setcases features dramatic high-mountain Pyrenean landscapes. You'll encounter pine forests, particularly black pine, transitioning to fescue grasslands above 2,300 meters. The area is characterized by majestic peaks, many exceeding 2,000 meters, and the source of the Ter River at Ulldeter.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking trails in Setcases?
A: Yes, while Setcases is known for its high-mountain routes, there are options suitable for families and beginners. The region offers numerous shorter local trails, such as "Camí dels Socarrats a Beduis" and "Camí dels Solans i el Catllar," which are generally less demanding. Out of the 94 available routes, 6 are rated as easy.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Setcases?
A: Yes, Setcases offers several excellent circular routes. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937158003/costabona-circular-from-setcases" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Costabona Circular from Setcases&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 12.6 km path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937136732/roca-colom-from-vallter-circular-through-the-natural-park-of-the-headwaters-of-ter-and-freser" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roca Colom from Vallter - Circular through the Natural Park of the Headwaters of Ter and Freser&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.9 km long.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Setcases?
A: The best time for hiking in Setcases is generally from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is more stable, and the higher mountain trails are typically free of snow. Autumn offers stunning foliage, while spring brings blooming wildflowers. Winter hiking is possible but requires specialized gear and experience due to snow and colder conditions.

Q: Are dogs allowed on hiking trails in Setcases and the Natural Park of the Headwaters of Ter and Freser?
A: Dogs are generally allowed on hiking trails in the Setcases area and within the Capçaleres del Ter i del Freser Natural Park. However, it is crucial to keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas, to protect wildlife and livestock. Always ensure you clean up after your pet.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see while hiking in Setcases?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the source of the Ter River at Ulldeter, visit the historic Ulldeter refuge hut (Spain's first mountain refuge), or enjoy panoramic views from various viewpoints like El Forat de l'Olla and La Creueta. The area is also home to majestic peaks such as Bastiments, Pic de la Dona, and Costabona. For dramatic geological formations, consider exploring the nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/260391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carança Corniche&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/873604" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Footbridges of the Carançà Gorge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is parking available for hikers in Setcases?
A: Yes, parking is available in and around Setcases, particularly in the village itself. For trails starting further afield, such as those near Vallter 2000, designated parking areas are usually provided. It's advisable to arrive early during peak season to secure a spot.

Q: Do I need permits to hike in the Parc Natural de les Capçaleres del Ter i del Freser?
A: Generally, permits are not required for day hiking on established trails within the Parc Natural de les Capçaleres del Ter i del Freser. However, for specific activities like camping or large organized groups, it's always best to check the official park regulations beforehand.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Setcases?
A: The hiking trails in Setcases are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the stunning high-mountain Pyrenean landscapes, the diverse range of routes for all abilities, and the well-maintained paths that lead to breathtaking viewpoints and natural landmarks.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or gorges to explore near Setcases?
A: Yes, the region around Setcases features impressive gorges. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/3703465/gorges-around-setcases" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;gorges around Setcases&lt;/a&gt;, which include highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/260391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carança Corniche&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/873604" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Footbridges of the Carançà Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. These areas offer unique natural formations and sometimes feature cascades, especially after rainfall or during snowmelt.
tips: The Pic de la Dona is a mountain of 2,702 meters that is located between the municipality of Setcases, in the region of Ripollès, and the municipality of Mentet, in the Conflent, the second in northern Catalonia

At 2,881 m, it is the highest peak on the ridge, reaching Puigmal.
It invites you to a longer ridge crossing.
The Marrana pass is a pass located at 2,530 m, located between the Bastiments peak and the Gra de Fajol, and which separates the Ter and Freser valleys at the time of the birth of both rivers.

Shelter with easy and quick access from the Vallter2000 car park. The nice guys, the comfortable beds, everything very clean and they have vegan options for food. From here there are good views of the Gra de Fajol and the Gra de Fajol Petit.
